comments.text: Subject: Portion Control Tasks — Action Required This Week  Hi [Name],  We're approaching the deadline on our Portion Control block and I need three specific tasks completed before end of [insert date]. These aren't optional — they feed directly into our recipe card standards and cost control, and the block can't close without them.  Here's what needs to happen:  Task 1 — Identify the ten highest-cost ingredients and define exact portions  What this means: Pull your top ingredients by unit cost — likely milk, syrups, proteins, specialty items. For each one, define the exact portion: grams, ml, pumps, shots. Not approximate. Exact.  When: Complete the identification by [Day 1]. Portions defined and written up by [Day 2].  With whom: You and [owner / head barista] together — this needs someone who knows the cost side and someone who knows the bar and kitchen reality. Don't do this alone.  Failure risk: The most common mistake here is using estimated portions rather than measured ones. Weigh and measure everything during a quiet period — not from memory, not from habit. If you skip this step, the numbers won't hold and the whole exercise loses its value.  Task 2 — Write portions onto prep cards and recipe cards (B054)  What this means: Every portion defined in Task 1 goes onto the relevant recipe card in clear, unambiguous language. "A splash" is not a portion. "20ml" is.  When: Complete within 24 hours of Task 1 being signed off.  With whom: You draft, I review before anything goes on the wall or into the folder.  Failure risk: Cards get written but filed away rather than placed at the point of use. These need to be physically at the station — bar, prep area, wherever the ingredient is used. If staff can't see it during service, it doesn't exist.  Task 3 — Demonstrate correct portioning to all kitchen and bar staff  What this means: A short, practical demonstration — not a meeting, not a briefing document. You physically show each team member the correct portion for each high-cost item. They watch, they repeat, you confirm.  When: Before the next full service after cards are in place. Ideally during a pre-shift window — 15 minutes is enough if you're prepared.  With whom: Every person who handles these ingredients. No exceptions. If someone misses it, catch them before their next shift — not after.  Failure risk: The demo happens once and is never reinforced. Portions drift back within a week. To prevent this: spot-check one or two items per shift for the first two weeks. Make it normal, not punitive — you're building a habit, not catching people out.  These three tasks are sequential — don't jump to Task 3 before Tasks 1 and 2 are solid. If anything is unclear or you hit a blocker, come to me immediately. The deadline is firm.  Thanks, [Your name]
comments.author: Lead Consultant

Address the single biggest time waster immediately
Ensure the menu board is clearly visible and readable from the queue
Create a standard order-taking sequence for all till operators
Train every front-of-house team member on the standard sequence
Practise the sequence during quiet periods until it becomes natural

tasks.title: Queue Communication Experience
tasks.description: Measure your peak queue times this week. Time how long customers wait from joining the queue to receiving their order during morning rush (7-10am) and lunchtime peak. If any customer waits more than 5 minutes to order or more than 8 minutes for a coffee something is wrong. Identify the bottleneck immediately — is it the till the espresso machine the food prep or the team capacity?

failure_modes.fm_title: Queue management and wait time failure

sub_actions.text: Establish the 30-Second Acknowledgement Rule for every customer
Post a reminder at the till: 'Look up. Make eye contact. Acknowledge.'
Assign acknowledgement responsibility to the till operator and backup team
Introduce the Queue Information Practice when more than 3 people are waiting
Train staff to read queue frustration signals: watch-checking, sighing, edging out
Practise proactive communication phrases during quiet periods
Note queue communication successes and misses in the daily log

tasks.title: Accessibility & Inclusivity Standards
tasks.description: Walk through your cafe as a customer today. Enter through the front door and experience the full journey: signage queueing ordering waiting collecting and seating. Note every friction point. Then consider accessibility: can a wheelchair user access the counter? Are there accessible toilets? Is your menu readable for someone with visual impairment? Fix any immediate barriers.

failure_modes.fm_title: Accessibility and inclusivity failure

sub_actions.text: Conduct an accessibility walk-through considering wheelchair, visual, hearing, and pushchair access
Create a prioritised barrier list categorised by fix timeframe
Implement immediate zero-cost fixes (furniture rearrangement, clear pathways)
Brief the team on inclusive service basics and appropriate language
Produce a large-print menu (minimum 14pt, high contrast, sans-serif font)
Update Google Business listing with accurate accessibility information
Ensure at least one table is wheelchair-accessible without requiring furniture to be moved

deliverables.name: Accessible Menu (large print and picture formats)

tasks.title: Experience Innovation & Differentiation
tasks.description: Walk through your cafe as a customer today. Enter through the front door and experience the full journey: signage queueing ordering waiting collecting and seating. Note every friction point. Then consider accessibility: can a wheelchair user access the counter? Are there accessible toilets? Is your menu readable for someone with visual impairment? Fix any immediate barriers.

failure_modes.fm_title: Accessibility and inclusivity failure

sub_actions.text: Conduct a 'What makes us different?' workshop with the full team
Interview five loyal regulars about why they choose your cafe
Identify the top three authentic differentiators from workshop and interview themes
Implement one immediate amplification of each strength
Audit the nearest five competitors for gaps and opportunities
Create a one-paragraph experience identity statement
Brief the team on the experience identity and how to articulate it

tasks.title: Return Visit Tracking
tasks.description: Pull your sales data and identify your return visit patterns. How many unique customers visit weekly? What is your ratio of regulars to new customers? If you cannot answer these questions you have no retention visibility. Start tracking return visits immediately through your POS system loyalty cards or simple observation. Identify your top 20 regulars and confirm your team knows them by name.

failure_modes.fm_title: Customer retention and return visit failure

sub_actions.text: Choose a tracking method appropriate to the cafe: loyalty cards, POS data, or manual tally
During one typical week, count recognised returning customers alongside total transactions
If POS supports customer profiles, pull a 3-month report on unique customers and visit frequency
Calculate baseline metrics: return rate, visit frequency, and revenue concentration
Share the baseline numbers with the team to build awareness of retention importance
Begin daily tracking of new versus returning customer ratio
Note any regulars who seem to have stopped visiting

deliverables.name: Return Visit Tracking Method (chosen and implemented)

If you cannot answer these questions you have no retention visibility. Start tracking return visits immediately through your POS system loyalty cards or simple observation. Identify your top 20 regulars and confirm your team knows them by name.

failure_modes.fm_title: Customer retention and return visit failure

sub_actions.text: Choose a loyalty programme format (stamp card recommended for initial launch)
Calculate programme economics: cost of reward versus marketing value per transaction
Design and print branded loyalty cards
Brief all till operators to offer the card proactively to every customer
Train staff on stamping procedure and redemption process
Begin tracking card issuance, stamp activity, and redemptions
Set up a simple redemption tracking system (tally sheet or POS integration)

deliverables.name: Loyalty Programme Design Document (format, rules, economics)
Branded Loyalty Card (printed or digital setup)

tasks.title: Regular Customer Recognition
tasks.description: Pull your sales data and identify your return visit patterns. How many unique customers visit weekly? What is your ratio of regulars to new customers? failure_modes.fm_title: Customer retention and return visit failure

sub_actions.text: Ask the team to identify the top 20 most frequent regular customers
Create a Regulars List with names (or descriptions), usual orders, and known details
Train staff on natural name-learning techniques: loyalty cards, order names
Begin using customers' names during interactions — once per visit, naturally
Acknowledge regulars immediately when they arrive: 'Morning, [name]!'
Start noting usual orders for regulars to enable proactive suggestions
Share the Regulars List with all front-of-house team members

tasks.title: Catering & Events Revenue
tasks.description: Identify revenue opportunities you are not currently pursuing. Could you offer catering for local offices? Sell retail coffee beans or merchandise? Develop an evening trade? Host events? Launch a subscription model? List five potential revenue streams and assess each for feasibility and margin. Start with the one that requires the least investment.

failure_modes.fm_title: Revenue diversification failure

sub_actions.text: Audit existing catering activity: requests received, pricing used, profitability, and lessons learned
Assess kitchen capacity and staff availability for catering production
Design an initial catering menu of 6-10 items that travel well and are operationally feasible
Price each item using cost-plus methodology (3-4x food cost) and benchmark against local competitors
Set minimum order value (50-75 pounds) and minimum lead time (48 hours)
Create a one-page catering menu for email and print distribution

deliverables.name: Catering Operations Process document (enquiry to follow-up)
Catering Pricing Calculator (cost-plus with margin analysis)
Catering Performance Tracker (monthly revenue, margins, repeat rate)

sub_actions.text: Assess retail opportunity by surveying 20 customers about interest in take-home products
Contact coffee roaster about retail packaging options, pricing, and branding
Select the initial retail product (typically 250g coffee bags of house blend)
Price retail coffee at 2.5-3x wholesale cost with competitive benchmarking
Set up a prominent retail display near the point of sale at eye level
Train staff with a natural one-line recommendation for retail products

deliverables.name: Retail Product Range plan with sourcing and pricing
Retail Pricing Model (cost-plus with margin analysis)

tasks.title: Daily Prep List Protocol
tasks.description: Review your daily operational discipline. Are prep lists being used? Is the waste log completed every day? Is the maintenance log up to date? Are daily targets communicated to the team? If any of these basic disciplines have lapsed recommit to them this week. Operational discipline is the foundation everything else is built on.

failure_modes.fm_title: Operational discipline and daily routine failure

sub_actions.text: Observe and document the current opening prep routine for five consecutive days
Create a Master Daily Prep List organised by category: food, beverage, station, housekeeping
Assign each task an estimated time, responsible role, and completion deadline
Print, laminate, and place the prep list on a clipboard at the main prep station
Implement daily sign-off — opening team ticks off tasks and signs the completed list
Retain completed lists for one week for reference and review

deliverables.name: Day-Specific Prep List Variants (weekday, Saturday, Sunday)
Prep Quantity Guide linked to expected covers

Implement daily sign-off by the closing team with immediate fault flagging
Schedule a baseline professional service for any equipment with unknown maintenance history

deliverables.name: Equipment Register with full details for every machine

Assign end-of-day waste total calculation to the closing manager
Calculate baseline waste-to-revenue percentage from first week of data

tasks.title: Cafe Values Definition
tasks.description: Can every team member articulate your cafe's values and standards? Ask them today. If responses are vague or inconsistent your values exist in your head but not in your culture. Check whether you have a staff handbook that documents expectations standards and policies. If not the gap between your intentions and your team's understanding is growing daily.

failure_modes.fm_title: Values articulation and alignment failure
Standards consistency and culture failure

sub_actions.text: Complete the Values Discovery exercise with honest written answers to the 6 core questions
Identify 3-5 core values from the patterns in the discovery answers
Write 3-5 specific behavioural examples for each value
Test the values by asking: would I fire someone who consistently violated this value? If not, it is not a core value
Share the draft values with your most trusted team member for a reality check
Refine the values based on feedback until they feel genuinely authentic

deliverables.name: Values Discovery exercise (written answers from owner)
Values Charter (single-page document with values, meanings, behaviours)
Team Values Workshop agenda and materials

tasks.title: Staff Handbook & Policies
tasks.description: Can every team member articulate your cafe's values and standards? Ask them today. If responses are vague or inconsistent your values exist in your head but not in your culture. failure_modes.fm_title: Values articulation and alignment failure
Standards consistency and culture failure

sub_actions.text: Audit all existing written employment documents and policy fragments
Draft Section 1 (Welcome and Company Overview) with owner's welcome message
Draft Section 2 (Employment Basics) covering pay, hours, probation, and notice periods
Create a handbook checklist tracking which sections are complete and which remain
Issue draft sections to all current staff with a timeline for completion
Ensure all staff have received a written statement of employment particulars as required by law

deliverables.name: Quick-Reference Policy Card for the staff area

tasks.title: Toxic Dynamics Intervention
tasks.description: Can every team member articulate your cafe's values and standards? Ask them today. If responses are vague or inconsistent your values exist in your head but not in your culture. failure_modes.fm_title: Values articulation and alignment failure

sub_actions.text: Define three to five specific unacceptable behaviours with concrete cafe-context examples
Post behavioural standards alongside team values in the staff area
Conduct an honest private assessment of any existing toxic dynamics in the current team
Categorise each identified dynamic by severity and determine appropriate response level
Address any dynamics causing active harm immediately using a direct private conversation
Brief all managers on the difference between performance issues and behavioural toxicity

deliverables.name: Toxic Behaviour Definition Guide with cafe-specific examples

tasks.title: Menu Pricing Strategy
tasks.description: Pull your revenue per hour data for the past four weeks. Identify your most and least productive hours. Is your average transaction value (ATV) growing or stagnant? When did you last review your prices? If it has been more than 12 months your prices are almost certainly too low given ingredient and wage inflation. Compare your prices to three local competitors this week.

failure_modes.fm_title: Revenue per hour and sales productivity failure

sub_actions.text: Calculate target selling price for every menu item using B111 cost data and target GP%
Compare target prices against current prices and flag items needing adjustment
Conduct a local competitor pricing audit covering the 5 nearest comparable cafes
Adjust prices where the gap is significant, phasing increases over 10%
Round prices to psychologically comfortable price points
Update all menus, EPOS, and delivery platform listings simultaneously
Communicate price changes to staff with confidence-building rationale

deliverables.name: Delivery Pricing Strategy with commission impact

tasks.title: Revenue Per Hour Tracking
tasks.description: Pull your revenue per hour data for the past four weeks. Identify your most and least productive hours. Is your average transaction value (ATV) growing or stagnant? When did you last review your prices? If it has been more than 12 months your prices are almost certainly too low given ingredient and wage inflation. Compare your prices to three local competitors this week.

failure_modes.fm_title: Revenue per hour and sales productivity failure

sub_actions.text: Check whether the EPOS system can generate hourly revenue reports
Generate or manually collect hourly revenue data for one full representative week
Create a simple table: hour, revenue, staff count, revenue per hour, revenue per labour hour
Plot the revenue-per-hour data on a chart to visualise the daily pattern
Identify the 3 highest and 3 lowest revenue hours
Calculate whether the lowest revenue hours cover their direct labour cost
Share the hourly revenue pattern with the management team

deliverables.name: Labour Schedule Template aligned to revenue pattern
Promotional Effectiveness report for low-revenue time slots

tasks.title: Upselling & Add-On Training
tasks.description: Pull your revenue per hour data for the past four weeks. Identify your most and least productive hours. Is your average transaction value (ATV) growing or stagnant? When did you last review your prices? If it has been more than 12 months your prices are almost certainly too low given ingredient and wage inflation. sub_actions.text: Calculate current average transaction value (ATV) and food attachment rate as baselines
Identify the top 5 natural upsell opportunities on the cafe's menu
Brief the team on the upselling approach, framing it as hospitality not sales
Give each team member one authentic suggestion phrase to use during service
Ensure the pastry and food display is visible and appealing from the ordering point
Identify the most effective point in the customer journey for suggestions (during drink preparation)
Begin tracking ATV daily using till data

deliverables.name: Average Transaction Value baseline and weekly tracker

tasks.title: Payroll Cost Optimisation
tasks.description: Calculate your payroll percentage — total staff costs divided by revenue. For cafes this should be between 30-40%. If it is above 40% you are either overstaffed during quiet periods or your revenue is too low. Review your cost structure: rent utilities insurance supplies. Identify your top three cost-reduction opportunities without compromising quality.

failure_modes.fm_title: Payroll cost management failure

sub_actions.text: Calculate current payroll cost as a percentage of revenue for the last 3 months
Include all oncosts: employer NI, pension, holiday accrual in the calculation
Map daily revenue against daily labour cost for each day of the week
Set a target labour percentage appropriate to the venue type
Identify the two or three shifts with the highest labour percentage
Make one or two conservative scheduling adjustments and measure impact
Review start and finish times for unnecessary early arrivals or late departures

deliverables.name: Labour Cost Analysis Spreadsheet (daily and weekly breakdown)
Flexible Shift Template Library (by day type)

tasks.title: Occupancy Cost Strategy
tasks.description: Calculate your payroll percentage — total staff costs divided by revenue. For cafes this should be between 30-40%. If it is above 40% you are either overstaffed during quiet periods or your revenue is too low. Review your cost structure: rent utilities insurance supplies. failure_modes.fm_title: Payroll cost management failure

sub_actions.text: Calculate total occupancy cost for the last 12 months (rent, rates, service charge, insurance, property maintenance)
Express occupancy cost as a percentage of revenue and compare against the 10-15% benchmark
Check business rates assessment on the Valuation Office Agency website
Verify eligibility for Small Business Rate Relief
Locate and review the lease document, noting key dates and clauses
Create a lease management calendar with rent review and break clause dates flagged 6 months ahead

deliverables.name: Occupancy Cost Analysis (annual breakdown with revenue percentage)

tasks.title: Exterior & Signage Refresh
tasks.description: Walk past your cafe from across the street. What does a potential customer see? Is your signage clear and inviting? Is the exterior clean — windows washed pavement swept A-board presentable? Step inside and look with fresh eyes: are surfaces clean tables cleared and floors spotless? First impressions are made in seconds and cannot be undone.

failure_modes.fm_title: Cleanliness and hygiene presentation failure

sub_actions.text: Walk the approach from every direction and photograph the exterior from a customer's perspective
Identify the top 5 exterior issues that most impact first impressions
Deep clean the exterior: windows, facade, door, outdoor furniture, pavement
Strip back cluttered window displays to a clean, inviting arrangement
Set up or refresh the A-frame board with a single compelling daily proposition
Ensure the entrance is obvious, clean, and welcoming

deliverables.name: Exterior Audit Report with photographs and recommended actions
